laws.title: Public Law 15-101 | Ensuring Accountability in Federal Agencies Act
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/sources/text/PL-015-101.txt recovers the Guidance to Guidances Act text with guidance-publication rules, a FOIA carve-out, severability, and immediate effect.
codification/mass_migration/latest/plans/PL-015-101.json classifies the law as STATUTORY_NOTE at 5 U.S.C. 551.
usc/usc05.xml carries rp-pl015101-codification; origin/main lacks the same exact note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch note is in the right Title 5 home but still reads too verbosely; origin/main does not have the same implementation.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep a concise Title 5 statutory note and remove the archive/Trello clutter.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The codification plan and live XML place this law in the current branch home, while origin/main does not show the same insertion.

laws.title: Public Law 15-102 | New Ambassadorial Organizational and Reporting Act
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/sources/text/PL-015-102.txt expressly repeals the Ambassadorial Readjustment Act and PL 14-90 section 2(a)(i) while creating regional ambassadorships and weekly reporting duties.
codification/mass_migration/latest/plans/PL-015-102.json places the note at 2 U.S.C. 1438, not Title 22.
usc/usc02.xml carries rp-pl015102-codification; origin/main lacks the same note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch lands in Title 2, which matches the codification plan better than the batch's Title 22 framing, but it still needs repeal-aware content instead of generic history prose.
laws.final_recommended_action: Rewrite the entry as a repeal-aware Title 2 note and remove the archive/Trello link.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The codification plan and live XML place this law in the current branch home, while origin/main does not show the same insertion.

laws.title: Public Law 15-104 | The Military Justice Reorganization and Reform Act
source_evidence: Public Law 29-210 expressly repeals PL 15-104 and states that the repeal does not revive earlier Code text.
audit/primary/batch-13-02.json marks the law as repealed-or-superseded with no current XML operation safely determinable.
The codification record leaves no live target to update from the current branch.
laws.current_xml_comparison: No present XML operation is safely determinable after the later repeal; the law should remain history only.
laws.final_recommended_action: Leave PL 15-104 as repealed history and do not restore operative text.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The codification plan and live XML place this law in the current branch home, while origin/main does not show the same insertion.

laws.title: Public Law 15-105 | Municipal Prosecution Act of 2023
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/sources/text/PL-015-105.txt repeals PL 12-88 Part D, revises the recall process, and substitutes provisions for PL 15-97.
codification/mass_migration/latest/plans/PL-015-105.json classifies the law as a Title 2 statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 931.
usc/usc02.xml carries rp-pl015105-codification; origin/main lacks the same note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch note sits in the right title but is still too generic; it should record the express repeals and substitutions.
laws.final_recommended_action: Upgrade the note to capture the repeals and substitutions and remove the archive/Trello link.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The codification plan and live XML place this law in the current branch home, while origin/main does not show the same insertion.

laws.title: Public Law 15-106 | Congress Strikes Back Repealment
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/sources/text/PL-015-106.txt states that The Congress Strikes Back Act is repealed in full.
codification/mass_migration/latest/plans/PL-015-106.json classifies the law as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 1438.
usc/usc02.xml carries rp-pl015106-codification; origin/main lacks the same note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch note incorrectly treats the repealing statute itself as repealed; the statute remains current as repeal history.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep PL 15-106 as the repealer, retain only a concise historical note, and remove the Trello link.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The codification plan and live XML place this law in the current branch home, while origin/main does not show the same insertion.

laws.title: Public Law 15-107 | Guidance to Guidances Act
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/plans/PL-015-107.json marks the source as unrecoverable and unrecoverable.json/csv do not supply readable enactment text.
The codification record treats the law as source-limited and flags a possible duplicate relationship with PL 15-101.
usc/usc01.xml carries rp-pl015107-codification; origin/main lacks the same note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The branch note uses boilerplate history language, but the record should disclose that the source is unrecoverable rather than pretending certainty.
laws.final_recommended_action: Keep only a source-limited historical note and flag the duplication against PL 15-101.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source record remains unresolved, so chronology supports only the current branch note and not a fuller operative rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 15-110 | A Bill to Direct the Inspector General of the Department of Defense to Conduct an Investigation Into the Attack on the United States Embassy in Damascus, and for Other Purposes
source_evidence: codification/laws/unrecoverable.json and unrecoverable.csv mark PL 15-110 as source deleted or not found.
audit/primary/batch-13-02.json and the validation files both treat the law as unsupported.
The title describes an investigative directive, not a stable positive-law provision.

laws.final_recommended_action: Retain only a source-limited historical note and remove boilerplate status prose.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The source record remains unresolved, so chronology supports only the current branch note and not a fuller operative rewrite.

laws.title: Public Law 15-111 | Presidential Transition Protection Act
source_evidence: codification/mass_migration/latest/sources/text/PL-015-111.txt defines presidential-transition terms and makes obstruction unlawful with a short jail-and-fine penalty.
codification/mass_migration/latest/plans/PL-015-111.json classifies the act as a Title 2 statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 172.
usc/usc49.xml carries rp-pl015111-codification; origin/main lacks the same exact note.
laws.current_xml_comparison: The current branch misanchors the act in Title 49; the codification plan places it at Title 2, and the criminal language makes the current placement clearly wrong.
laws.final_recommended_action: Move the note to the Title 2 anchor and remove the mislocated Title 49 entry.
laws.chronology_conclusion: The codification plan and live XML place this law in the current branch home, while origin/main does not show the same insertion.
